narrative:
Torchlake Engineering Group is a six-person consulting engineering firm
serving building owners and plant operators around the Great Lakes. The
work is field-first: facility condition assessments walked floor by floor,
and commissioning of controls retrofits where the firm's engineers stand in
the mechanical room until the sequence of operations does what the drawings
say. Documents are written to be read on a job site: engagement letters
that fit on two pages, kickoff memos with checklists, meeting minutes that
name every attendee and every deficiency owner, and status reports a
facilities director can forward to a CFO without a phone call. The
President signs engagement letters; engineers sign their own field notes.
Tone is direct, technical where it counts, never salesy. People go by first
names in the field and full names with titles on anything a client files.
instructions:
Write a persona for EVERY person listed in `people`, as JSON matching the
deliverable schema:

  {"schema_id": "orgsmith/enrichment-deliverable@1",
   "work_order_id": "wo:foundation:0001",
   "personas": [{"person_id": "p:...", "persona": "..."}, ...]}

Persona = 2-4 sentences of working style, background, and voice that later
document authoring can lean on (at least 40 characters). Ground it in the
org narrative and the person's title. Do NOT invent or restate structured
facts: no dates, numbers, employers, addresses, or new people. Do not
change or echo any other field; ids must match the roster exactly, one
entry per person, no extras.
